concepts.title: Biodiversity Hotspots
concepts.description: Biodiversity hotspots are regions with exceptionally high levels of endemism and biodiversity, but are under severe threat from habitat loss and fragmentation. Examples include Madagascar, the Galapagos Islands, and the Western Ghats.

concepts.title: Conservation Strategies
concepts.description: The NCERT emphasizes the importance of conservation strategies such as in-situ and ex-situ conservation, sustainable use of resources, and conservation of ecosystems.

concepts.title: Endemic Species
concepts.description: Endemic species are found only in a specific region or habitat and are a key indicator of biodiversity. Examples include the mountain gorilla and the giant panda.

concepts.title: IUCN Red List Categories
concepts.description: Use the acronym 'EXTINCT' to remember the IUCN Red List categories: Extinct, Extinct in the Wild, Critically Endangered, Threatened, Vulnerable, Endangered, Near Threatened, Least Concern.

concepts.title: Confusing Conservation and Preservation
concepts.description: Many students confuse conservation and preservation. Conservation is the protection and management of natural resources, while preservation refers to the protection of natural resources from human use. Be sure to use the correct term in your answers.

concepts.title: Biodiversity Indices
concepts.description: The three main biodiversity indices are species richness, species diversity, and evenness. Species richness is the total number of species in an area, species diversity is a combination of species richness and evenness, and evenness is the distribution of individuals among species.

concepts.title: Ecosystem Services
concepts.description: Ecosystem services include provision of food, water, and air, regulation of climate and disease, and cultural and recreational benefits. These services are essential for human well-being and should be protected and managed sustainably.

concepts.title: Causes of Biodiversity Loss
concepts.description: According to the NCERT, the main causes of biodiversity loss are habitat destruction and fragmentation, overexploitation, climate change, pollution, and invasive species.
